---
title: "Interval STL Runtime Assurance"
description: "Runtime assurance for uncertain systems using interval signal temporal logic."
categories: [Safety, Formal Methods, Hybrid Systems, Research]
---

**Links:**

::: {.d-flex .gap-2 .mb-3}
[View Publications Page](../../publications/){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary}
[Back to Projects](../){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}
:::

This project studies runtime assurance for uncertain systems using interval signal temporal logic (iSTL). Instead of checking temporal requirements against a single nominal trajectory or collapsing uncertainty into a crude worst-case margin, the goal is to reason directly about bounded uncertainty while still preserving meaningful guarantees.

The paper, with Leslie Baird and Samuel Coogan, is currently under revision for IFAC *Nonlinear Analysis: Hybrid Systems* (NAHS). It sits on the more formal-methods side of the same safe-autonomy thread that runs through the rest of the work on this site.

**Key ideas:**

- Use interval signal temporal logic to encode temporal requirements under bounded uncertainty
- Connect formal temporal specifications with runtime-assurance reasoning
- Keep guarantees informative without defaulting to extreme conservatism
- Frame uncertainty-aware safety analysis in a way that is compatible with hybrid-systems thinking

**Academic foundation:**

| Venue | Status |
|---|---|
| IFAC *Nonlinear Analysis: Hybrid Systems* (NAHS) | Submitted, under revision |

**Built around:** Runtime assurance · Temporal logic · Interval analysis · Hybrid systems
